steps.title: Identify the dual requirement
steps.content: The claim states Lady Chiltern is perceived as both extremely virtuous and unforgiving. The correct quotation must illustrate both of these contrasting qualities, not just one.

steps.title: Match the quotation to both qualities
steps.content:
Choice C — "[Lady Chiltern] does not know what weakness or temptation is. I am of clay like other men. She stands apart as good women do—pitiless in her perfection—cold and stern and without mercy." is correct because it pairs “pitiless in her perfection”—which fuses her moral excellence with her harsh, unfeeling nature—with “cold and stern and without mercy,” explicitly capturing the unforgiving aspect.

The other quotations fail to demonstrate both qualities: some highlight only virtue or sensibility, while another presents the speaker's cynical philosophy rather than Lady Chiltern's actual character.

That matches Choice C — "[Lady Chiltern] does not know what weakness or temptation is. I am of clay like other men. She stands apart as good women do—pitiless in her perfection—cold and stern and without mercy.".
